session_log_buffer.c(3) Library Functions Manual session_log_buffer.c(3)

NAME

session_log_buffer.c

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct session_log_buffer
Internal circular buffer structure.

Typedefs


typedef struct session_log_buffer session_log_buffer_t
Internal circular buffer structure.

Functions


session_log_buffer_t * session_log_buffer_create (void)
void session_log_buffer_destroy (session_log_buffer_t *buf)
void session_log_buffer_clear (session_log_buffer_t *buf)
void session_log_buffer_append (session_log_buffer_t *buf, const char *message)
size_t session_log_buffer_get_recent (session_log_buffer_t *buf, session_log_entry_t *out_entries, size_t max_count)

Typedef Documentation

typedef struct session_log_buffer session_log_buffer_t

Internal circular buffer structure.

Function Documentation

void session_log_buffer_append (session_log_buffer_t * buf, const char * message)

Definition at line 65 of file session_log_buffer.c.

65                                                                                {
66   if (!buf || !message) {
67     // Fail silently - this is called FROM the logging system
68     // Using SET_ERRNO here would cause infinite recursion
69     return;
70   }
71 
72   mutex_lock(&buf->mutex);
73 
74   size_t pos = atomic_load_u64(&buf->write_pos);
75   uint64_t seq = atomic_fetch_add_u64(&buf->sequence, 1);
76 
77   SAFE_STRNCPY(buf->entries[pos].message, message, SESSION_LOG_LINE_MAX);
78   buf->entries[pos].sequence = seq;
79 
80   atomic_store_u64(&buf->write_pos, (pos + 1) % SESSION_LOG_BUFFER_SIZE);
81 
82   mutex_unlock(&buf->mutex);
83 }




References atomic_fetch_add_u64(),
    atomic_load_u64(), atomic_store_u64(),
    session_log_buffer::entries, session_log_buffer::mutex,
    session_log_buffer::sequence, and
    session_log_buffer::write_pos.






void
  session_log_buffer_clear (session_log_buffer_t * buf)


Definition at line 45 of file
  session_log_buffer.c.


45                                                          {
46   if (!buf) {
47     return;
48   }
49 
50   mutex_lock(&buf->mutex);
51 
52   // Reset write position and sequence
53   atomic_store_u64(&buf->write_pos, 0);
54   atomic_store_u64(&buf->sequence, 0);
55 
56   // Clear all entries
57   for (size_t i = 0; i < SESSION_LOG_BUFFER_SIZE; i++) {
58     buf->entries[i].message[0] = '\0';
59     buf->entries[i].sequence = 0;
60   }
61 
62   mutex_unlock(&buf->mutex);
63 }






References atomic_store_u64(),
    session_log_buffer::entries, session_log_buffer::mutex,
    session_log_buffer::sequence, and
    session_log_buffer::write_pos.


Referenced by terminal_screen_log_clear().






session_log_buffer_t * session_log_buffer_create (void
  )


Definition at line 23 of file
  session_log_buffer.c.


23                                                       {
24   session_log_buffer_t *buf = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(session_log_buffer_t), session_log_buffer_t *);
25   if (!buf) {
26     return NULL;
27   }
28 
29   atomic_store_u64(&buf->write_pos, 0);
30   atomic_store_u64(&buf->sequence, 0);
31   mutex_init(&buf->mutex, "log_buffer");
32 
33   return buf;
34 }






References atomic_store_u64(),
    session_log_buffer::mutex, mutex_init(),
    session_log_buffer::sequence, and
    session_log_buffer::write_pos.


Referenced by terminal_screen_log_init().






void
  session_log_buffer_destroy (session_log_buffer_t * buf)


Definition at line 36 of file
  session_log_buffer.c.


36                                                            {
37   if (!buf) {
38     return;
39   }
40 
41   mutex_destroy(&buf->mutex);
42   SAFE_FREE(buf);
43 }






References session_log_buffer::mutex, and
    mutex_destroy().


Referenced by terminal_screen_log_destroy().






size_t
  session_log_buffer_get_recent (session_log_buffer_t * buf,
  session_log_entry_t * out_entries, size_t max_count)


Definition at line 85 of file
  session_log_buffer.c.


85                                                                                                                     {
86   if (!buf || !out_entries || max_count == 0) {
87     // Fail silently - called from display code that handles 0 gracefully
88     // Using SET_ERRNO here could cause recursion if error logging is enabled
89     return 0;
90   }
91 
92   mutex_lock(&buf->mutex);
93 
94   size_t write_pos = atomic_load_u64(&buf->write_pos);
95   uint64_t total_entries = atomic_load_u64(&buf->sequence);
96 
97   size_t start_pos = write_pos;
98   size_t entries_to_check = SESSION_LOG_BUFFER_SIZE;
99 
100   if (total_entries < SESSION_LOG_BUFFER_SIZE) {
101     start_pos = 0;
102     entries_to_check = write_pos;
103   }
104 
105   size_t count = 0;
106   for (size_t i = 0; i < entries_to_check && count < max_count; i++) {
107     size_t idx = (start_pos + i) % SESSION_LOG_BUFFER_SIZE;
108     if (buf->entries[idx].sequence > 0) {
109       memcpy(&out_entries[count], &buf->entries[idx], sizeof(session_log_entry_t));
110       count++;
111     }
112   }
113 
114   mutex_unlock(&buf->mutex);
115   return count;
116 }






References atomic_load_u64(),
    session_log_buffer::entries, session_log_buffer::mutex,
    session_log_buffer::sequence, session_log_buffer::write_pos,
    and write_pos.


Referenced by log_search_gather_and_filter_logs(), and
    terminal_screen_render().
